reasoning: Liu Zige's world record of 2:01.81 in the women's 200m long-course butterfly was set in 2009 during the supersuit era and is widely considered one of the most unbreakable records in swimming. The fastest textile performance in history is Summer McIntosh's 2:03.03 at the Paris 2024 Olympics, which remains 1.22 seconds slower than Liu's mark. In a 200m race at the absolute limits of human performance, bridging a gap of over 1.2 seconds in a little over a year is exceptionally unlikely, even for a generational talent like McIntosh. Consequently, it is highly probable Liu Zige will retain the record through early 2026.
